title: Centaurium chanetii
intro: Centaurium chanetii, also known as Chanet's centaury, is a plant species in the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and reddish-brown stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium chanetii include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Centaurium chanetii is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Centaurium chanetii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a perennial herb native to China. The classification of Centaurium chanetii includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its rank as a species within the binomial nomenclature system. The plant is further classified into its order Gentianales, and its family Gentianaceae. Within the family Gentianaceae, Centaurium chanetii belongs to the genus Centaurium.
